revision.text:
[[ja:吾妻神社 (横浜市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = {{ruby|Azuma Shrine|あづまじんじゃ|qq=}} |image = [[File:Azuma shrine Yokohama.jpg|thumb]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=240|coord={{coord|35|25|20.9|N|139|40|3.3|E|qq=}}|qq=}} |所在地 = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|qid=Q38283|lt=Yokohama City|1=Yokohama}}{{ill|Naka-ku, Yokohama|en|Naka-ku, Yokohama|qid=Q1141068|lt=Naka Ward|1=Naka-ku, Yokohama}}{{ill|Honmokuhara|ja|本牧原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honmokuhara|1=Honmoku-hara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q48764464}}29-18 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|20.9|N|139|40|3.3|E|display=inline,title|qq=}} |地図 = {{Location map|Yokohama|lat_deg=35|lat_min=25|lat_sec=20.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=40|lon_sec=3.3|label={{ill|Azuma Shrine|ja|吾妻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuma Shrine|qq=}}|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|qq=}} |祭神 = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Japanese Imperial Hero|1=Yamato Takeru}} |社格 = Village shrine |創建 = {{ill|Bunna|en|Bunna|qid=Q1146252|lt=Bunwa|1=Bunna}}3rd year ({{ill|1354|en|1354|qid=Q6118|lt=1354 AD|1=1354}}) or {{ill|Tenna|en|Tenna|qid=Q1145289|lt=Tenwa|1=Tenna}}3rd year ({{ill|1683|en|1683|qid=Q7651|lt=1683 AD|1=1683}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} [[File:Azuma shrine Yokohama Torii.jpg|240px|thumb|Shrine Torii]] '''Azuma Shrine''' (吾妻神社, あづまじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|qid=Q38283|lt=Yokohama City|1=Yokohama}}, Naka Ward of Yokohama. == History == It was founded either in {{ill|1354|en|1354|qid=Q6118|lt=1354 AD|1=1354}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Bunna|en|Bunna|qid=Q1146252|lt=Bunwa|1=Bunna}}) or in {{ill|1683|en|1683|qid=Q7651|lt=1683 AD|1=1683}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Tenna|en|Tenna|qid=Q1145289|lt=Tenwa|1=Tenna}}). According to the Bunwa account, it was established by {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|qid=Q1328796|lt=Shinden Yoshinori|1=Nitta Yoshisada}}’s retainer {{ill|Shinozuka Shigehiro|ja|篠塚重広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinozuka Shigehiro|1=Shinozuka Shigehiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11603659}}. The evidence is an inscribed statue—depicting an ancient man in traditional attire—bearing the inscription “Bunwa Kōgo 3rd year, January 17, Foundation Established by Shinkinhei Shigehiro”. However, this statue was destroyed in the {{ill|Yokohama Air Raid|ja|横浜大空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Air Raid|1=Bombing of Yokohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11542960}} of {{ill|1945|en|1945|qid=Q5240|lt=1945 AD|1=1945}} (Showa 20). Alternatively, the Tenwa account holds that a fisherman named {{ill|Yoshitaifu|ja|吉太夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshitaifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} discovered a floating statue at sea while fishing. This statue had been housed at the {{ill|Azuma Shrine (Kisarazu)|ja|吾妻神社 (木更津市)|lt=Azuma Shrine|lt_ja=吾妻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuma Shrine (Kisarazu)|1=Azuma Shrine (Kisarazu City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11416641}} in Azuma Village, located in {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|qid=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province|1=Kazusa Province}}, {{ill|Botaru District|ja|望陀郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Botaru District|1=Mōda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11517166}}, (present-day {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|Kisarazu|en|Kisarazu|qid=Q335427|lt=Kisarazu City|1=Kisarazu}}{{ill|Azuma (Kisarazu)|ja|吾妻 (木更津市)|lt=Azuma|lt_ja=吾妻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuma (Kisarazu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), but it was washed away by a {{ill|Tsunami|en|Tsunami|qid=Q8070|lt=tsunami|1=Tsunami}}. Yoshitaifu later became a Shinto priest and founded this shrine to enshrine the statue. A nearby temple, {{ill|Tendoku-ji (Yokohama)|ja|天徳寺 (横浜市)|lt=Tendoku-ji|lt_ja=天徳寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tendoku-ji (Yokohama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which served as a {{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, is also associated with the legend.{{ill|Hiroshi Inaba|ja|稲葉博|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshi Inaba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 『{{ill|Kanagawa Temples and Shrines: Their Establishment and Origins (Kamome Bunko – Kanagawa Furusato Series 18)|ja|かながわの寺と社 その成立と縁起|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Temples and Shrines: Their Establishment and Origins (Kamome Bunko – Kanagawa Furusato Series 18)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』, {{ill|Kanagawa Gōdō Shuppan|ja|神奈川合同出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Gōdō Shuppan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1984, pp. 81–82[http://www.osannomiya-hie.or.jp/azuma.html {{ill|Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine|qq=}}] Currently, Azuma Shrine is administered as a concurrent shrine of {{ill|O-Sanno no Miya Hie Shrine|ja|日枝神社 (横浜市南区)|lt=O-Sanno no Miya Hie Shrine|lt_ja=お三の宮日枝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/O-Sanno no Miya Hie Shrine|1=O-Sanno no Miya Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11509533}}, located in Minami Ward and Sannōchō, Yokohama. == Festivals == * {{ill|January 17|en|January 17|qid=Q2264|lt=January 17|1=January 17}} (Spring Festival) * {{ill|July 17|en|July 17|qid=Q2729|lt=July 17|1=July 17}} (Summer Festival) == Transportation == * {{ill|Transit bus|en|Transit bus|qid=Q1373492|lt=Local Bus|1=Transit bus}}: a 3-minute walk from {{ill|Honmokuhara Bus Stop|ja|本牧原停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honmokuhara Bus Stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. {{-|qq=}} == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help|qq=}} {{Reflist|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Hiroshi Inaba|ja|稲葉博|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshi Inaba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 『{{ill|Kanagawa Temples and Shrines: Their Establishment and Origins (Kamome Bunko – Kanagawa Furusato Series 18)|ja|かながわの寺と社 その成立と縁起|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Temples and Shrines: Their Establishment and Origins (Kamome Bunko – Kanagawa Furusato Series 18)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』, {{ill|Kanagawa Gōdō Shuppan|ja|神奈川合同出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Gōdō Shuppan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1984 {{translated page|ja|吾妻神社 (横浜市)|version=103441135|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:吾妻神社 (横浜市)]] [[ja:吾妻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134926948}} [[Category:本牧]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:横浜市中区の建築物]] [[Category:横浜市中区の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]]
